Yaml

---
title: "Chapter 05: Foundations of GLMs – Families, Links, and Log-Concave Likelihoods"
author: "Kjell Nygren"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Chapter 05: Foundations of GLMs – Families, Links, and Log-Concave Likelihoods}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

# 1. Conceptual Overview
- What are exponential families?
- Role of canonical link functions
- Why log-concavity matters: implications for optimization and sampling
 
# 2. GLMs in Classical vs Bayesian Contexts
- Brief comparison of glm() vs glmb() workflows
- How prior specification interacts with family/link choices

# 3. Supported Families and Link Functions in glmb
- Table of currently implemented families (e.g., Gaussian, Poisson, Gamma, Binomial)
- Supported link functions for each family
- Notes on compatibility with Prior_Setup()

# 4. Specifying Families and Links in Practice
- Examples using glmb() and Prior_Setup()
model <- glmb(y ~ x, family = gaussian(link = "identity"), prior = Prior_Setup(...))
- Commentary on how link choice affects interpretation and estimation

# 5. Log-Concavity and Estimation Procedures
- Why log-concave likelihoods simplify envelope construction
- Implications for Gibbs sampling and subgradient methods
- When non-log-concave models require fallback strategies

# 6. Pedagogical Notes and Textbook Mappings
- Cross-reference to canonical GLM theory (e.g., McCullagh & Nelder, Gelman et al.)
- Suggestions for further reading or simulation exercises
